title: QA flow automation — auto-merge + auto-pickup of merged sprint PRs

stories.title: .github/workflows/qa-intake-auto-merge.yml — auto-merge qa-intake PRs
stories.summary: .github/workflows/qa-intake-auto-merge.yml runs on pull_request_target [opened, reopened, synchronize, labeled] when the PR carries the `qa-intake` label. Steps: (1) checkout PR head; (2) diff against base to enumerate touched files; (3) regex-guard each path against `^(backlog/qa-tickets/|examples/.*/playtests/qa-proposed/|qa-artifacts/)` — any other path fails the job; (4) `gh pr checks --watch --fail-fast` blocks until CI is terminal-green; (5) `gh pr merge --squash --auto --delete-branch`. Per-PR concurrency group prevents racing on resync events.
verification: acceptance: QA opens a PR with the `qa-intake` label that only touches `backlog/qa-tickets/`. CI runs. Within ~1 minute of green CI, the PR is squash-merged automatically; main picks up the tickets without dev relay.
Path-guard step rejects any PR touching engine/, examples/**/src/, tests/, etc. — even if labelled qa-intake — with a clear `::error::` message
If CI fails the action exits non-zero and leaves the PR open for QA to fix

stories.title: QA scaffold helper prints the `gh pr create --label qa-intake` recipe
stories.summary: scripts/backlog/qa-ticket.mjs's `new` subcommand now ends with a 6-line stderr block printing the exact canonical sequence: `git checkout -B qa-intake/YYYY-WW`, stage commands for QA-writable dirs, commit + push, and the `gh pr create --label qa-intake --title ...` invocation. ISO-week derived from today's UTC date so the branch name is always right. docs/qa/agent.md's filing-loop section gains step 7 explicitly documenting that the `qa-intake` label is required for auto-merge.
verification: acceptance: `npm run qa:ticket -- new "<title>" --severity major --type bug` ends with stderr `[qa-ticket] gh pr create --base main --label qa-intake --title "qa-intake: ..." ...`. QA can copy verbatim.
docs/qa/agent.md step 7 documents the label requirement

stories.title: Sprint PRs labelled `sprint` for QA discoverability
stories.summary: CLAUDE.md §QA-Workflow sprint-close section now spells out the canonical `gh pr create --base main --label sprint --title "S<NN>: ..." --body-file <(node scripts/backlog/pr-body.mjs)` command so every sprint PR from S094 forward carries the `sprint` label. Convention only — no script wrapping, since pr-body.mjs renders only the body and the gh call is one line. Existing/closed PRs unchanged.
verification: acceptance: Next sprint PR I open via the documented command carries `sprint` label; `gh pr list --label sprint --state merged` returns it after merge, and `npm run qa:next-pr` picks it up.
CLAUDE.md sprint-close docs updated with the canonical command

stories.title: scripts/backlog/qa-next-pr.mjs — find the most recently merged sprint PR
stories.summary: scripts/backlog/qa-next-pr.mjs uses `gh pr list --label sprint --state merged` to enumerate dev sprint PRs, walks every qa-ticket file (live AND archived) to build a set of already-reviewed PR numbers (via the ticket's foundInPr field), and returns the newest unreviewed entry. Reads the matching sprint JSON to extract every implemented story's `acceptance:` line so the QA agent immediately sees its verification contract. Two output modes: human (default, multi-line printout) and `--json` for tooling. Exits 0 with `Caught up` when nothing is unreviewed. New npm script `qa:next-pr`.
verification: acceptance: `npm run qa:next-pr` on a state where PR #1000 is the newest unreviewed sprint PR prints PR meta + every `acceptance:` line from the sprint JSON; after QA files a ticket with foundInPr=1000, re-running it returns the next-older sprint PR (or 'Caught up').
Returns exit 0 + 'Caught up' when no unreviewed PR exists

+4 unit tests in tests/unit/qa-next-pr.test.ts using a PATH-stubbed `gh` binary: caught-up text path, --json caught-up path, newest-mergedAt selection, acceptance lines surfaced from a real sprint JSON

stories.title: QA agent runs qa:next-pr automatically when invoked
stories.summary: docs/qa/agent.md 'How to launch' kickoff prompt now starts the QA session with `npm run qa:next-pr` and tells the agent to exit cleanly on `Caught up`. .claude/agents/qa-reviewer.md gains an explicit Step 0 in the per-PR job list calling out qa:next-pr as the first action. Together: a fresh QA session needs no user nudging — it lands directly on the next sprint PR's acceptance criteria.
verification: acceptance: A fresh QA session pasting the Option-A kickoff prompt from docs/qa/agent.md lands on the most recent unreviewed sprint PR (or exits with 'caught up') without any further user prompting.
.claude/agents/qa-reviewer.md step 0 documents qa:next-pr first; docs/qa/agent.md kickoff text aligned

stories.title: Move QA artefacts to a tracked `qa-artifacts/` directory (closes QA-2026-05-20-001)
stories.summary: QA filed QA-2026-05-20-001: `Notes/` in .gitignore matches `notes/` on macOS's case-insensitive filesystem, blocking artefact commits. Tried `!notes/qa/` re-include first — confirmed git refuses to re-include children of an ignored parent directory. Final fix: dropped the broken re-include, retired the `notes/qa/` path entirely in favour of a dedicated **tracked** `qa-artifacts/` directory at the repo root. .gitignore gains a comment documenting the limitation so a future agent doesn't repeat the mistake. Schemas, docs, and the QA subagent definition all updated via bulk sed; only `qa-artifacts/README.md` is committed in this story — agents create per-ticket subdirs as needed.
verification: acceptance: `git check-ignore -v qa-artifacts/anything.png` returns nothing (path is tracked). Writing a file there + `git add` stages it.

stories.title: Promote QA-2026-05-20-{001,002} from S092 dogfood into this sprint
stories.summary: First dogfood loop closed. `npm run qa:promote --into S094` promoted both tickets from PR #102 into S094 as proper stories (BUG-NOTES-QA-IS-GITIGNORED-Q-001 + BUG-NOTES-QA-WORKFLOW-DESIGN-001). Source files archived under backlog/qa-tickets/archive/S094/. Both promoted stories immediately deferred via `backlog:defer` with reasons pointing at their fix commits — 87d0fc8 for the gitignore fix in this sprint, 0015475 for the design-doc move in S93. Discovered + fixed a too-conservative original constraint in promote-qa.mjs (active sprint rejected); now accepts both `pending` and `active`.
verification: acceptance: `npm run qa:promote -- --into S094` ran cleanly; both tickets landed as stories; sources archived under backlog/qa-tickets/archive/S094/. Stories now status='deferred' with reasons pointing at the upstream fix commits.
promote-qa.mjs target-sprint guard accepts both pending + active sprints
Existing tests/unit/qa-promote.test.ts coverage stays green (none of the 7 tests relied on rejection of active)

stories.title: notes/qa/** is gitignored — QA artifact files cannot be committed
stories.summary:
S092 design §7 designates notes/qa/** as QA's writable output directory for screenshots, console dumps and repro artifacts. However, .gitignore contains the pattern Notes/ and git config core.ignoreCase=true, which causes git to silently ignore all notes/ paths as well (case-insensitive match). Any file QA writes to notes/qa/ will never be tracked, making the artifact capture part of the workflow (screenshots, log excerpts) permanently broken. Companion to QA-2026-05-20-002.

QA repro
--------
severity: major · type: doc · filed: 2026-05-20T10:30:00Z · PR #101 · under sprint S092

Steps:
  1. Open .gitignore at repo root — locate the pattern `Notes/` on line 30.
  2. Run: echo 'notes/qa/test.txt' | git check-ignore --stdin -v
  3. Observe: git reports `.gitignore:30:Notes/  notes/qa/test.txt` — the file is ignored despite the lowercase path.
  4. Run: git config core.ignoreCase — returns `true`, confirming case-insensitive .gitignore matching.
  5. Attempt to create notes/qa/QA-2026-05-20-001/screenshot.png and git add it — git silently skips the file.
Expected: notes/qa/** is a tracked path so QA agents can commit screenshots, console dumps, and other repro artifacts alongside ticket JSON files.
Actual:   .gitignore pattern Notes/ with core.ignoreCase=true matches notes/ too. Every file written to notes/qa/ is silently ignored by git. The field screenshot in qa-ticket.schema.json can reference a path but the file will never land in the repository.

Logs:
.gitignore:30:Notes/	notes/qa/test.txt (from git check-ignore --stdin -v)

Source ticket: QA-2026-05-20-001
stories.deferredReason: Fixed in same sprint by QA-FIX-NOTES-GITIGNORE (commit 87d0fc8) — qa-artifacts/ now tracked.

stories.title: notes/qa-workflow-design.md lives in gitignored Notes/ — Read-first link broken on fresh clones
stories.summary:
The S092 planning sprint's acceptance criterion and docs/qa/agent.md (landing in S093) both point readers to notes/qa-workflow-design.md as the primary design reference. The file was written to Notes/qa-workflow-design.md, which is gitignored. On any fresh clone or CI system the file is absent, making the Read-first link a dead reference. The actionable QA workflow is fully documented in the committed docs/qa/*.md triad, so the practical impact is limited to discoverability and onboarding friction. Root cause is shared with QA-2026-05-20-001.

QA repro
--------
severity: minor · type: doc · filed: 2026-05-20T10:31:00Z · PR #101 · under sprint S093

Steps:
  1. Clone the repo fresh (after S093 merges) or check out main on a machine that does not have the local Notes/ folder.
  2. Open docs/qa/agent.md and find the Read-first section.
  3. Follow the link to notes/qa-workflow-design.md.
  4. Observe: the file does not exist — ls notes/qa-workflow-design.md returns 'No such file or directory'.
Expected: notes/qa-workflow-design.md is either committed to the repo or the docs/qa/agent.md Read-first section clearly marks it as an optional local-only reference.
Actual:   The file exists only in the gitignored Notes/ folder on the author's machine. Fresh clones see a dead link with no explanation.

Source ticket: QA-2026-05-20-002
stories.deferredReason: Fixed in S93 commit 0015475 — design doc moved to docs/qa/design.md.
